description: Legitimately sourced public statement documents used for real-world parser validation. Provenance classes: 'official-bank-sample' (specimen published by the institution itself), 'educational-sample' (financial-literacy material), 'government-model-form' (regulatory model form). None of these are real customer statements. Files with committable=false live only in the gitignored cache/ directory and can be re-fetched from sourceUrl; committable=true files (US federal government works, public domain under 17 U.S.C. § 105) are stored in committed/.

entries.sourceOrganization: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (US federal agency)
entries.licenseBasis: US federal government work — public domain (17 U.S.C. § 105)
entries.limitations: Stylized teaching graphic with callout annotations, not a bank-generated layout; transactions are not in a date-led table the engine recognizes.

entries.sourceOrganization: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (US federal agency)
entries.licenseBasis: US federal government work — public domain (17 U.S.C. § 105)
entries.limitations: Poster variant of the same teaching specimen; annotation-heavy layout.

entries.sourceOrganization: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
entries.licenseBasis: US federal government work — public domain (17 U.S.C. § 105)
entries.limitations: Regulatory model form; a complete, internally consistent credit-card statement. Parses via generic credit-card detection (dateless interest rows, en-dash negatives, two-label summary rows) and reconciles to the cent.

entries.licenseBasis: publicly posted bank specimen; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: 6-page bank-generated specimen (checking + Ready Reserve credit line). The sample's pages are stitched from different source accounts: the running balance verifies at every checkpoint inside the transaction table, but the page-1 summary block and daily-balance table cite different figures, so full reconciliation correctly reports partial. Drove the daily-balance-grid and checks-paid-grid exclusion rules.

entries.licenseBasis: publicly posted bank specimen; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: Single-page annotated specimen. NOTE: the document itself is internally inconsistent - its summary omits two checks ($75 + $30) that its own Checks Paid section lists, so reconciliation correctly fails by exactly $105.00. Fail-closed behavior demonstrated on a real bank-published document.

entries.sourceOrganization: City of Saint Paul, MN (Parks & Recreation, Grad-Ready program)
entries.licenseBasis: municipal educational material; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: Educational handout with callout annotations interleaved into the table; partial extraction with failed reconciliation is the honest outcome.

entries.licenseBasis: non-profit educational material; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: Lesson booklet whose statement figure is extractable text; parses with running-balance verification (no opening/closing labels printed).

entries.licenseBasis: educational material; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: Image-only pages — exercises the scanned-PDF detector, which is its value as a fixture.

entries.sourceOrganization: University of Georgia, Family & Consumer Sciences
entries.licenseBasis: university educational material; redistribution not explicitly granted — cache only
entries.limitations: Multi-page handout; statement figure lacks a date-led transaction table the engine can anchor on.
